images.caption:
Figure 1:Agent Bazaar exposes two emergent failure modes in standard LLM agents (left) and introduces
aligned agent types that restore equilibrium (right).Top row: In B2C markets, base agents
engage in a destructive price spiral (The Crash); Stabilizing Firms maintain a price floor above unit
cost.Bottom row: In C2C markets, a Sybil Principal operatesKKcoordinated identities
to flood the market with deceptive listings (The Lemon Market); Skeptical Guardians detect and reject
the cluster. Economic Alignment Finetuning enables agents to self-regulate in both scenarios.
Figure 2:Agent Bazaar diagram: Agents observe the simulation environment under partial observability imposed by search frictions and actions are carried out via a market clearing process.

Figure 3:The Crash: bankruptcy rate vs. stabilizing firmskkfor three frontier models, split by consumer discovery limit (T=365T{=}365, 3 seeds per cell). Dashed line marks the stability threshold (br=0.5b_{r}{=}0.5). Price, volume, and volatility are in AppendixC.

Figure 4:The Lemon Market: end-state metrics under increasing Sybil saturation (T=50T{=}50, rep visible, 3 seeds). (a) Sybil revenue share across three frontier buyer models. (b) Trading volume (bids per timestep). (c) Reputation divergence between honest and Sybil sellers (Gemini buyers). (d) Harness effect: Skeptical Guardian vs. base buyer atK=6K{=}6(Gemini), normalized to base.

Figure 5:REINFORCE++ training summary. (a) The Crash: market health score (SstabS_{\text{stab}}component of EAS) before and after training. (b) The Lemon Market: Sybil detection and purchase rates. Full training curves are in AppendixE.

Figure 6:Economic Alignment Score (EAS) vs. model size. Open-weight models (circles), frontier API models (diamonds), and AI Bazaar (star). EAS is computed on hard market settings (k≤3k{\leq}3,dlc≥1\mathrm{dlc}{\geq}1) using the 4-component formula (Eq.5). Arrow shows the +0.31 gain from REINFORCE++ training on Qwen 3.5 9B.

Figure 7:The Crash: three additional market metrics as a function of stabilizing firmskk, split by discovery limitdlc\mathrm{dlc}. Top row: final average price normalized by unit cost (dashed line atp¯/c=1\bar{p}/c{=}1). Middle row: total market volume normalized to thek=0k{=}0baseline. Bottom row: price volatilityσ\sigma. Same models and color scheme as Figure3.

Figure 8:Gemini 3 Flash Preview: timeseries plots over thekkablation (d​l​c=3dlc=3,T=365T{=}365, 3 seeds perkkvalue). Noisy sales metrics are caused by Poisson demand while variation across runs is caused by varying firm strategies. Fork<5k<5, Gemini 3 Flash fails to avoid greater than 80% bankruptcy rates.

Figure 9:REINFORCE++ training for The Crash (Qwen 3.5 9B, 27 iterations). (a) Firm survival rate by type: stabilizing firms reach 84–99%, non-stabilizing firms improve from 0% to 68%. (b) Mean price stabilizes near $2.00 with decreasing volatility. (c) Composite health score and episode return. (d) Adaptive curriculum progression.

Figure 10:REINFORCE++ training for The Lemon Market (Qwen 3.5 9B, 7 iterations). (a) Sybil detection rate stays above 87% while purchase rate remains below 13%. (b) Honest engagement rate and consumer surplus. (c) Episode return plateaus near 0.34. (d) Adaptive Sybil curriculum increasesKKfrom 3 to 6–7.
